Freedom_Fighter/Abdul Kalam.webp: The 'Missile Man of India' and 11th President, who led India's space program and missile development, inspiring youth.
Freedom_Fighter/Bhagat Sinh.webp: A heroic young revolutionary who founded the HSRA and sacrificed his life, popularizing 'Inquilab Zindabad'.
Freedom_Fighter/Bipin Chandra Pal.webp: A key leader of the Lal-Bal-Pal triumvirate who pioneered the Swadeshi boycott of foreign goods.
Freedom_Fighter/Captain Laxmi.webp: Led the Rani of Jhansi Regiment, the all-women combat wing of Netaji's Indian National Army (INA).
Freedom_Fighter/Chandrashekhar Azad.webp: A fierce revolutionary leader of the HSRA who vowed never to be captured alive by the British forces.
Freedom_Fighter/Chitranjan Das.webp: Prominent lawyer and freedom fighter who co-founded the Swaraj Party and mentored young Subhash Chandra Bose.
Freedom_Fighter/Dr Hedgevar.webp: A doctor and nationalist who founded the RSS in 1925 to culturally organize society for national independence.
Freedom_Fighter/Dr Radhakrishnan.webp: Renowned philosopher, statesman, and India's first Vice President, whose birthday is celebrated as Teachers' Day.
Freedom_Fighter/Dr. BR Ambedkar.webp: The chief architect of the Constitution of India, who spent his life fighting for social equality and civil rights.
Freedom_Fighter/Gandhiji_01.webp: Father of the Nation, who led India's independence movement using non-violent resistance and Satyagraha campaigns.
Freedom_Fighter/Gopal Krishna Gokhle.web: A senior nationalist leader of the Congress and social reformer who served as political mentor to Mahatma Gandhi.
Freedom_Fighter/Indira Gandhi.webp: First female Prime Minister of India, who led the nation through critical integration and the 1971 war.
Freedom_Fighter/Jhansi Ki Rani.webp: The brave Queen of Jhansi who fought British forces on horseback during the historic Rebellion of 1857.
Freedom_Fighter/Kasturba Gandhi.webp: Dedicated political activist and wife of Mahatma Gandhi, who led civil rights movements and faced frequent arrests.
Freedom_Fighter/Lal Bahadur Shashtri.web: Second Prime Minister of India, who led the 1965 war and coined the national slogan 'Jai Jawan Jai Kisan'.
Freedom_Fighter/Lala Lajpat Rai.webp: The 'Lion of Punjab' who led protests against the Simon Commission and pioneered national self-reliance.
Freedom_Fighter/Lokmanya Tilak.webp: A pioneer of Swaraj who declared 'Swaraj is my birthright!' and mobilized the masses using public festivals.
Freedom_Fighter/Maharana Pratap.webp: Legendary Rajput King of Mewar who fought Mughal expansionism in the Battle of Haldighati to protect Mewar.
Freedom_Fighter/Mangal Pandey.webp: A sepoy of the 34th Bengal Native Infantry whose rebellion sparked the Indian Uprising of 1857.
Freedom_Fighter/Rajguru.webp: Fearless revolutionary of the HSRA who participated in the Saunders case alongside Bhagat Singh and Sukhdev.
Freedom_Fighter/Ravindranath Tagor.webp: Nobel laureate poet who composed the national anthem 'Jana Gana Mana' and renounced his British knighthood.
Freedom_Fighter/Sardar Patel.webp: The 'Iron Man of India' who united over 560 princely states into the newly independent Indian Union.
Freedom_Fighter/Sarojini Naidu.webp: The 'Nightingale of India', a poet and leading freedom fighter who led the Dharasana Satyagraha.
Freedom_Fighter/Shivaji Maharaj.webp: The great Maratha King who established 'Hindavi Swarajya' (self-rule) and fought foreign empires.
Freedom_Fighter/Subhash Chandra Bose.web: Netaji, who founded the Azad Hind Fauj (INA) to launch an armed war against the British for complete independence.
Freedom_Fighter/Sukhdev.webp: A key revolutionary leader of the HSRA who organized national youth fronts and was executed with Bhagat Singh.
Freedom_Fighter/Swami Dayanand Saraswati: A Vedic scholar and founder of Arya Samaj who was among the first to call for 'Swarajya' (Self-Rule).
Freedom_Fighter/Swami Vivekanand.webp: A spiritual leader who represented India at the Parliament of Religions, inspiring national pride and youth duty.
Freedom_Fighter/Tantiya Bhil.webp: A tribal revolutionary hero known as the 'Robin Hood of India' who fought British forest exploitation.
Freedom_Fighter/Veer Savarkar.webp: An influential revolutionary leader, writer, and thinker who co-founded the Abhinav Bharat Society.
